title: Family Welcome To The Future, Where AI Grandma Raises The Kid You Bought From The Baby Bank By: Elle Purnell November 19
raw_response: {"rating": 6, "lean": "Right", "explanation": "This is an opinion/editorial that strongly advances a socially conservative, religiously grounded critique of AI and assisted reproductive technologies. The author consistently champions traditional family structure and natural-law theology while denigrating IVF, surrogacy, embryo selection, and transgender medicine, leading to a Right rating of +6. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author endorses a normative model that men and women are designed to form families and raise children together, and criticizes arrangements involving single or same-sex parents as intentionally creating motherless or fatherless children; (2) Religious natural-law framing pervades the piece, invoking Eden, playing God, the natural order, and an omnipotent God to argue that rebellion against physical laws is rebellion against divine law; (3) Assisted reproductive technologies are condemned as eugenic and dehumanizing, with embryos described as perishing and surrogacy portrayed as ripping children from birth mothers, without balancing perspectives or countervailing data; (4) Culture-war language equates gender transition with mutilation and defends the sex binary as part of a natural design, positioning the piece within right-leaning critiques of transgender medicine; (5) AI memorial tools are depicted with mocking, pejorative terms such as necromancy, soulless characters, and Tamagotchi, reinforcing a moral panic narrative rather than a neutral assessment; (6) The headline and framing characterize IVF/surrogacy as buying a baby from a bank, signaling opposition to commodification in reproduction aligned with conservative ethics; (7) Selective sourcing and omissions: reliance on ideologically aligned or anecdotal evidence (e.g., a religious news outlet’s embryo-loss math, celebrity examples, and cautionary tales) with no inclusion of supportive expert voices for ART or AI memorialization; (8) Editorial linking promotes further critical content about the fertility industry, amplifying a one-sided, right-leaning narrative."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is an opinion/editorial that strongly advances a socially conservative, religiously grounded critique of AI and assisted reproductive technologies. The author consistently champions traditional family structure and natural-law theology while denigrating IVF, surrogacy, embryo selection, and transgender medicine, leading to a Right rating of +6. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author endorses a normative model that men and women are designed to form families and raise children together, and criticizes arrangements involving single or same-sex parents as intentionally creating motherless or fatherless children; (2) Religious natural-law framing pervades the piece, invoking Eden, playing God, the natural order, and an omnipotent God to argue that rebellion against physical laws is rebellion against divine law; (3) Assisted reproductive technologies are condemned as eugenic and dehumanizing, with embryos described as perishing and surrogacy portrayed as ripping children from birth mothers, without balancing perspectives or countervailing data; (4) Culture-war language equates gender transition with mutilation and defends the sex binary as part of a natural design, positioning the piece within right-leaning critiques of transgender medicine; (5) AI memorial tools are depicted with mocking, pejorative terms such as necromancy, soulless characters, and Tamagotchi, reinforcing a moral panic narrative rather than a neutral assessment; (6) The headline and framing characterize IVF/surrogacy as buying a baby from a bank, signaling opposition to commodification in reproduction aligned with conservative ethics; (7) Selective sourcing and omissions: reliance on ideologically aligned or anecdotal evidence (e.g., a religious news outlet’s embryo-loss math, celebrity examples, and cautionary tales) with no inclusion of supportive expert voices for ART or AI memorialization; (8) Editorial linking promotes further critical content about the fertility industry, amplifying a one-sided, right-leaning narrative.
